title: ERROR-line field extraction to pipe-delimited
prompt: You are given server log lines. Each line has the form:
From every line whose level is exactly ERROR, extract three fields:
2. the service name (the third whitespace-separated token),
3. the numeric value that appears as code=<number> inside the message.
Output one line per matched ERROR entry, in the SAME order they appear in the log,
Do not include non-ERROR lines. Do not add headers, commentary, or trailing text.
2026-03-01T08:12:04Z INFO  auth-svc      user login ok uid=4471
2026-03-01T08:12:05Z ERROR billing-svc   charge failed code=5012 uid=4471
2026-03-01T08:12:09Z ERROR ledger-svc    write conflict code=4090 txn=88123
2026-03-01T08:12:11Z ERROR billing-svc   charge failed code=5012 uid=4472
Output ONLY the result lines between <answer> and </answer> tags, nothing else.
difficulty_rationale: Pure pattern extraction with an explicit output template; no reasoning, no ambiguity. A correct low-effort read of the spec suffices. Expected near-ceiling pass at every tier; anchors the easy end of the curve.
